pattern: Building an answer one character at a time, where position i deliberately differs from the i-th string's own i-th character, guarantees the result cannot equal any string in the list -- each one is ruled out at a different position.
hints: You need a binary string of length n that is guaranteed to differ from every one of the n given strings, and you get to build it however you like.

Make sure your answer differs from the first string somewhere, from the second string somewhere else, and so on -- if you pick one dedicated position per string to guarantee a mismatch, you never need to check the others.

Walk i from 0 to n-1: look at character i of the i-th input string, and set character i of your answer to the opposite bit. The result is guaranteed to disagree with every input string at its own diagonal position.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two other two-bit strings
four close variants differing only in low bits
maximum length, sixteen distinct sixteen-bit strings

stress.description: random distinct n-bit strings at sizes up to the sixteen-element cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 16))
    seen = set()
    result = []
    attempts = 0
    while len(result) < n and attempts < 10000:
        attempts += 1
        s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
        if s not in seen:
            seen.add(s)
            result.append(s)
    return [result]
stress.note: distinct random n-bit strings, generated without replacement up to n of the 2^n possibilities
